index.title: Hush Meet - Auto-Mute Chrome Extension for Google Meet
index.tagline: A Chrome extension that automatically mutes your microphone on Google Meet when you're not speaking, preventing background noise from reaching other participants.
index.feature_auto_mute: Auto-unmutes when speech is detected, auto-mutes after you stop speaking
index.mode_auto_off_desc: Unmute only by Meet manual control or shortcut, auto-mute after speech (for noisy environments)
index.feature_mic_button: Popup mic button — mute/unmute directly from the popup without closing it. In Push-to-Talk mode, the button works as hold-to-talk (press and hold to unmute, release to mute)
index.feature_shortcut: Customizable shortcut key (default: Ctrl+Shift+M) — available in all modes except Off
index.feature_independent_audio: Independent audio monitoring (separate mic stream from Google Meet)
index.feature_noise_gate: Voice-weighted noise gate — emphasizes human voice frequencies (300Hz–3kHz), reducing false triggers from keyboard, fan, and ambient noise
index.feature_meter: Real-time audio level meter & spectrum analyzer
index.feature_manual_mute: Manual mute safety — if you click Meet's mute button directly, the extension automatically switches to Off mode to avoid interference
index.feature_mic_selector: Microphone device selector — choose which mic to use
index.feature_themes: 4 visual themes (Default / Analog Radio / Boombox / Retro Future)
index.feature_toolbar_icon: Mode-aware toolbar icon — icon color and badge change based on active mode
index.feature_language: English & Japanese language support with manual switching
index.feature_games: Bored in a meeting? Retro-future mini-games included (Breakout / Space Invaders / 2048 / Flappy Bird). Fair warning: game sound effects can trigger auto-unmute. Set your sensitivity just right and enjoy the thrill of not getting caught.
index.how_it_works_flow: [Mic Input] → [Web Audio API (RMS)] → [Speech Detection] → [Meet Mute Button Control]
index.how_it_works_desc: A separate microphone stream monitors audio levels. When speech is detected, the extension automatically clicks the Meet mute button to unmute. When silence returns, it re-mutes after a configurable grace period.
index.setting_shortcut_desc: Keyboard shortcut for mute control (all modes except Off)
index.manual_mute_desc: When you manually mute or unmute via the Meet mute button, the extension automatically switches to Off mode. This prevents the extension from unexpectedly turning your mic back on after you intentionally muted it.
index.manual_mute_observer: Monitors the Meet mute button via MutationObserver
index.manual_mute_distinguish: Distinguishes extension-initiated vs. user-initiated mute changes
index.manual_mute_tracking: Manual mute button click → extension switches to Off mode
index.manual_mute_mode: Use the shortcut key (Ctrl+Shift+M) to control mute while keeping the extension active
index.getting_started_1: Install from the Chrome Web Store
index.getting_started_3: Click the Hush Meet icon in the toolbar
index.getting_started_6: Use the mic button in the popup to mute/unmute without closing it — in Push-to-Talk mode, press and hold the button to talk
index.getting_started_7: You can also use the shortcut key (Ctrl+Shift+M) on the Meet tab to toggle mute — clicking Meet's mute button directly will switch the extension to Off mode

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.overview_text: Hush Meet (hereinafter "this extension") is a Chrome extension that detects speech during Google Meet calls and automatically toggles mute. This extension respects user privacy to the fullest extent and uses only the minimum amount of data necessary.
privacy.data_collection_text: This extension does not collect or transmit any personal data.
privacy.mic_access_text: This extension requires microphone access for speech detection. Regarding the audio data obtained from the microphone:
privacy.mic_realtime: Audio data is processed in real time only on your device
privacy.mic_no_record: Audio data is never recorded or stored
privacy.mic_no_server: Audio data is never sent to external servers
privacy.mic_rms_only: Only the audio volume level (RMS value) is used for computation; the content of the audio is never analyzed
privacy.local_storage_text: This extension uses the Chrome Storage API to store the following settings locally:
privacy.local_storage_note: This data is stored only within your browser and is never transmitted externally.
privacy.website_access_text: This extension operates only on https://meet.google.com. Content scripts are not executed on any other websites.
privacy.third_party_text: This extension does not provide any data to third parties. It contains no analytics tools, tracking code, or advertisements.
privacy.open_source_text: The source code of this extension is available on GitHub. If you have any questions about how data is handled, you can review the source code directly.
privacy.contact_text: If you have questions about this privacy policy, please contact us via GitHub Issues.
privacy.changes_text: This privacy policy may be updated as needed. If there are significant changes, we will notify you on this page.

architecture.title: Technical Documentation
architecture.subtitle: Architecture and design of Hush Meet
architecture.file_content_script: Content Script (state machine, mode control, shortcuts)
architecture.content_script_desc: This script is injected into the Google Meet page. Its main responsibilities are:
architecture.audio_engine_desc: Uses the Web Audio API AnalyserNode to measure microphone input volume in real time.
architecture.state_machine_desc: A finite state machine with 5 states manages the speech state.
architecture.state_diagram:
IDLE ──(mode other than Off selected)──► MUTED ──(volume > speechThreshold)──► UNMUTING ──► SPEAKING
                     ▲                                                │
                     │                              (volume < silenceThreshold)
                     │                                                │
                     └──(graceTimer expired)── GRACE ◄────────────────┘
                                                │
                                         (volume > silenceThreshold)
                                                │
                                                ▼
                                            SPEAKING

* If the user manually toggles the Meet microphone, the mode stays the same and only the state follows.
architecture.operating_modes_desc: Four modes control how state transitions behave. Off is the control-disabled mode.
architecture.shortcut_ptt: Push-to-Talk: Unmute only while key is held (hold operation)
architecture.shortcut_customize: The shortcut key can be freely changed from the popup settings.
architecture.asymmetric_desc: Different thresholds for unmuting and muting prevent chattering (frequent toggling).
architecture.asymmetric_diagram:
speechThreshold (speech start)  = 0.025  (default)
silenceThreshold (silence)      = speechThreshold × 0.5 = 0.0125

  Volume
   ▲
   │  ┄┄┄┄┄┄┄┄┄ speechThreshold (0.025) ┄┄┄┄  ← Unmute when exceeded
   │
   │  ┄┄┄┄┄┄┄┄┄ silenceThreshold (0.0125) ┄┄  ← Grace period starts when below
   │
   └──────────────────────────────────────────► Time
architecture.mute_button_desc: Detects the Google Meet mute button using multiple strategies:
architecture.mute_button_state: Mute state is determined from the data-is-muted attribute or the aria-label text content.
architecture.popup_desc: A settings UI built with React 19. Synchronizes state with the Content Script in real time via the Chrome Storage API.
architecture.popup_mode_selector_desc: Switch between Off / Auto / Auto-Off / Push-to-Talk
architecture.popup_status_desc: Shows the current state (muted/speaking/grace) with a dot indicator
architecture.popup_level_meter_desc: Displays microphone input level in real time (with threshold markers)
architecture.popup_mic_desc: Manual selection of the microphone to use
architecture.data_flow_desc: The Popup and Content Script do not communicate directly; they communicate bidirectionally via Chrome Storage API change events.
architecture.limitation_rms: Voice detection is RMS (volume) based, so it may false-trigger on loud ambient noise (mitigated by using Auto-Off mode)
architecture.limitation_ui_change: Google Meet UI changes may break mute button detection
architecture.limitation_clipping: The very beginning of speech may be cut off (adjustable via threshold settings)
architecture.limitation_focus: The shortcut key only works when the Google Meet tab is focused
architecture.future_noise_gate: Voice-weighted noise gate for improved detection accuracy
architecture.future_buffering: Pre-speech buffering to reduce beginning-of-utterance clipping
architecture.future_auto_detect: Auto-detection logic to follow Google Meet UI changes
architecture.future_silero: Silero VAD (ML-based) replacement for higher detection accuracy
